message.content:
Test file: src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Failing test: "should handle negative coordinates"
Expected behavior: The Game of Life implementation should work correctly with negative x and y coordinates
Current error: Test already passes
Implementation file: src/game-of-life.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I need to understand the current state of the tests and implementation to proceed with the Green phase.

I can see the test "should handle negative coordinates" already exists at line 120-127 of the test file, and the implementation already supports negative coordinates through the coordinate parsing and neighbor calculation logic.

Let me run the tests to confirm the current state:

content.content:
$ vitest run

 RUN  v1.6.1 /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_10-30-59_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_haiku-4-5-no-thinking

 ✓ src/game-of-life.spec.ts  (11 tests) 4ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
   Start at  10:45:41
   Duration  153ms (transform 26ms, setup 0ms, collect 25ms, tests 4ms, environment 0ms, prepare 41ms)

content.text:
Perfect! All tests are passing, including the "should handle negative coordinates" test. This test was already implemented and passing. Since there is no failing test to fix, the Green phase is complete - the implementation already correctly handles negative coordinates.

The implementation supports negative coordinates through:
1. The `parseCoordinate` function that correctly parses "x,y" strings with negative numbers
2. The `getNeighborCells` function that adds offsets to any coordinates (including negative ones)
3. The core logic that treats all coordinates uniformly regardless of sign

The test at line 120-127 validates this by using cells with negative coordinates (-1,-1), (-1,0), (0,-1) and correctly expects a new cell to be born at (0,0) when it has exactly 3 neighbors.

Green phase complete. Proceeding to Refactor phase.
